text: AEGIS HEALTH INSURANCE COMPANY LTD. Comprehensive Health Insurance Policy Policy Name: AegisShield Comprehensive Health Plan Policy Number: AHIC/2024/COMP/001892 Sum Insured: n5,00,000 (Five Lakhs) Policy Period: 01-April-2024 to 31-March-2025 SECTION 1: DEFINITIONS 1.1 Hospitalization: Admission to a hospital for a minimum period of 24 consecutive hours for treatment of an illness or injury, as an inpatient. 1.2 Pre-existing Disease: Any condition, ailment, injury or disease that is diagnosed

of an illness or injury, as an inpatient. 1.2 Pre-existing Disease: Any condition, ailment, injury or disease that is diagnosed before the commencement of the policy or first manifested during the 48 months prior to the date of the policy. 1.3 Day Care Treatment: Medical treatment and/or surgical procedure which requires less than 24 hours of hospitalization due to technological advancement. 1.4 Network Hospital: A hospital which has a tie-up arrangement with the Company for direct claims

due to technological advancement. 1.4 Network Hospital: A hospital which has a tie-up arrangement with the Company for direct claims settlement (cashless facility). 1.5 Room Rent: The amount charged per day by a hospital for accommodation including nursing charges and service charges. 1.6 Co-payment: A cost-sharing requirement where the insured pays a specified percentage of the admissible claim amount. SECTION 2: SCOPE OF COVERAGE 2.1 In-Patient Hospitalization The Company shall indemnify the

specified percentage of the admissible claim amount. SECTION 2: SCOPE OF COVERAGE 2.1 In-Patient Hospitalization The Company shall indemnify the insured person for reasonable medical expenses incurred during hospitalization for treatment of any illness or injury, subject to the terms and conditions of this policy. Coverage includes room charges, ICU charges, surgeon fees, anesthetist fees, diagnostic tests, medicines, and consumables. 2.2 Pre-Hospitalization Expenses Medical expenses incurred up

room charges, ICU charges, surgeon fees, anesthetist fees, diagnostic tests, medicines, and consumables. 2.2 Pre-Hospitalization Expenses Medical expenses incurred up to 30 days prior to hospitalization shall be covered, provided they are directly related to the illness/injury for which hospitalization was required. 2.3 Post-Hospitalization Expenses Medical expenses incurred up to 60 days after discharge from the hospital shall be covered, provided they are directly related to the illness/injury

up to 60 days after discharge from the hospital shall be covered, provided they are directly related to the illness/injury for which hospitalization was required. 2.4 Day Care Procedures The following day care procedures are covered: Dialysis, Chemotherapy, Radiotherapy, Cataract Surgery, Angiography, Lithotripsy, Tonsillectomy, and any other procedure requiring less than 24 hours of hospitalization that is accepted by IRDAI as a day care procedure. 2.5 Ambulance Charges Reasonable ambulance

than 24 hours of hospitalization that is accepted by IRDAI as a day care procedure. 2.5 Ambulance Charges Reasonable ambulance charges up to n3,000 per hospitalization for transportation to the nearest hospital. SECTION 3: LIMITS AND SUB-LIMITS

Benefit Limit Sum Insured n5,00,000 per annum Room Rent (General Ward) n3,000 per day Room Rent (Private Room) n5,000 per day ICU Charges n10,000 per day Ambulance Charges n3,000 per hospitalization Pre-hospitalization Up to 30 days Post-hospitalization Up to 60 days Co-payment 10% of admissible claim (age > 60: 20%) Annual Aggregate Deductible Nil 3.1 Room Rent Clause: If the room rent charges exceed the specified sub-limit, all other admissible claim components shall be proportionately reduced

Rent Clause: If the room rent charges exceed the specified sub-limit, all other admissible claim components shall be proportionately reduced in the same ratio as the room rent sub-limit bears to the actual room rent charged. For example, if the actual room rent is n10,000/day and the sub-limit is n5,000/day, all other charges will be reduced by 50%. 3.2 Proportionate Deduction: In case of room rent exceeding the sub-limit, the formula for proportionate deduction is: Eligible Amount = (Room Rent

Deduction: In case of room rent exceeding the sub-limit, the formula for proportionate deduction is: Eligible Amount = (Room Rent Sub-limit / Actual Room Rent) × Total Claim Amount SECTION 4: EXCLUSIONS 4.1 Permanent Exclusions (Never Covered) (cid:127) Cosmetic or aesthetic treatment/surgery of any kind, including but not limited to rhinoplasty, liposuction, hair transplant, and skin resurfacing (cid:127) Dental treatment or surgery, unless requiring hospitalization (cid:127) Cost of

limited to rhinoplasty, liposuction, hair transplant, and skin resurfacing (cid:127) Dental treatment or surgery, unless requiring hospitalization (cid:127) Cost of spectacles, contact lenses, and hearing aids (cid:127) Treatment for obesity/weight management including bariatric surgery (cid:127) Infertility treatment including IVF, IUI, and related procedures (cid:127) Self-inflicted injuries or attempted suicide (cid:127) Treatment arising from alcohol or substance abuse (cid:127) Injuries

IUI, and related procedures (cid:127) Self-inflicted injuries or attempted suicide (cid:127) Treatment arising from alcohol or substance abuse (cid:127) Injuries sustained during participation in hazardous activities or adventure sports (cid:127) Treatment for any condition arising from war, nuclear contamination, or terrorism (cid:127) Experimental or investigational treatment not approved by regulatory authorities (cid:127) LASIK and refractive eye surgery (cid:127) Sex change surgery and

Experimental or investigational treatment not approved by regulatory authorities (cid:127) LASIK and refractive eye surgery (cid:127) Sex change surgery and related treatments (cid:127) Treatment for congenital external anomalies unless life-threatening 4.2 Waiting Period Exclusions Initial Waiting Period: Any illness contracted during the first 30 days of the policy (except accidental injuries) shall not be covered. Specific Disease Waiting Period: The following conditions shall be covered only

the policy (except accidental injuries) shall not be covered. Specific Disease Waiting Period: The following conditions shall be covered only after a continuous waiting period of 24 months from the policy start date: Cataract, Joint Replacement, Hernia, Hysterectomy, Kidney Stones, Gallstones, Sinusitis, Piles/Fistula, Tonsillectomy, and benign prostatic hypertrophy. 4.3 Pre-existing Disease Clause Pre-existing diseases shall be covered after a continuous waiting period of 48 months (4 years)

hypertrophy. 4.3 Pre-existing Disease Clause Pre-existing diseases shall be covered after a continuous waiting period of 48 months (4 years) from the date of inception of the first policy with the Company. This includes conditions such as [TABLE] Benefit | Limit Sum Insured | n5,00,000 per annum Room Rent (General Ward) | n3,000 per day Room Rent (Private Room) | n5,000 per day ICU Charges | n10,000 per day Ambulance Charges | n3,000 per hospitalization Pre-hospitalization | Up to 30 days

per day ICU Charges | n10,000 per day Ambulance Charges | n3,000 per hospitalization Pre-hospitalization | Up to 30 days Post-hospitalization | Up to 60 days Co-payment | 10% of admissible claim (age > 60: 20%) Annual Aggregate Deductible | Nil

Diabetes, Hypertension, Cardiac conditions, Asthma, COPD, Thyroid disorders, and any other condition that was known to the insured prior to the policy inception. The insured must disclose all pre-existing conditions at the time of proposal. SECTION 5: CLAIMS PROCESS 5.1 Cashless Claims For cashless treatment at a network hospital, the insured must inform the Company or TPA at least 48 hours prior to planned hospitalization, or within 24 hours of emergency admission. Pre-authorization will be

or TPA at least 48 hours prior to planned hospitalization, or within 24 hours of emergency admission. Pre-authorization will be processed within 2 hours for emergencies and 12 hours for planned admissions. 5.2 Reimbursement Claims For reimbursement claims, the insured must submit the following documents within 15 days of discharge: Original hospital bills, Discharge summary, Investigation reports, Prescription copies, Pharmacy bills, Policy copy, and a duly filled claim form. Claims shall be

bills, Discharge summary, Investigation reports, Prescription copies, Pharmacy bills, Policy copy, and a duly filled claim form. Claims shall be settled within 30 days of receipt of all required documents. 5.3 Required Documents (cid:127) Duly filled and signed claim form (cid:127) Original hospital final bill with detailed breakup (cid:127) Discharge summary / Certificate (cid:127) All investigation and diagnostic reports (cid:127) Doctor's prescriptions and consultation notes (cid:127)

detailed breakup (cid:127) Discharge summary / Certificate (cid:127) All investigation and diagnostic reports (cid:127) Doctor's prescriptions and consultation notes (cid:127) Pharmacy/Medicine bills with prescriptions (cid:127) Photo ID proof of the insured (cid:127) Copy of the insurance policy (cid:127) FIR copy (in case of accidental claims) (cid:127) Pre-authorization letter (for cashless claims) This is a specimen policy document for demonstration purposes.
